source_jobs.title: Deployment Strategist
source_jobs.description:
About Hanover Park

Hanover Park http://hanoverpark.com/ builds AI systems that run the financial operations of the world’s most sophisticated investment firms. As an AI-enabled service https://www.emcap.com/thoughts/the-death-of-deloitte-ai-enabled-services-are-opening-a-whole-new-market and fund administrator https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fund_administration, we’ve become the way fund CFOs adopt AI. We've raised $31 million from Emergence https://www.emcap.com/ (investor in Salesforce/Zoom), Susa https://www.susaventures.com/ (investor in Robinhood/Flexport) and angel investor fund CFOs from Bain Capital https://www.baincapital.com/ ($180B), Clearlake https://clearlake.com/ ($100B), CD&R https://www.cdr.com/ ($80B), and Hellman & Friedman https://hf.com/ ($80B).

Our mission is to be the financial infrastructure to orchestrate how the world’s capital is operated, governed and allocated. Stripe for payments. Ramp for expenses. Hanover Park for investments. In the next decade, the AI systems we build will advise $100B investment firms on their largest acquisitions and eventually unlock alpha for investors.




ABOUT THE ROLE

You're the operator who turns a signed contract into a raving fan. You're embedded with billion-dollar CFOs as they navigate the biggest technology transformation in fund finance. This isn't "implementation." It isn't "customer success." It's the closest thing to founding a company while inside one - you are the person who makes sure the world's most sophisticated CFOs actually get value from AI.

To date, deployment has been owned by our CEO Chris and our founding team. The pipeline is overflowing, the product wins demos, and the single biggest constraint is now the gap between "signed" and "evangelist." That's where you come in. As the Founding Deployment Strategist, you'll work shoulder-to-shoulder with Chris and our engineering team - owning live customers, building the playbook as you go, and setting the bar that the next ten deployment hires.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Owning customer outcomes from kickoff through go-live and beyond - turning every signed deal into a reference account

 - Writing specs with engineering when a customer needs a feature to unlock value, and shipping it together in days

 - Building SOPs from scratch for workflows that don't yet exist - capital calls, NAV close, audit prep, GP reporting

 - Training CFOs and their finance teams on how to actually use Hanover Park's MCP within Claude/GPT for their daily workflows - building skills, prompts, and agents alongside them

 - Sitting in customer Slack channels as the trusted operator they DM at 11pm

 - Partnering directly with product and engineering. When a customer needs something, you figure out whether it ships this week or never

Your only outcome metric: time to turn a signed customer into a raving fan.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 2+ years of forging hard things into existence - as a founder, FDE, IB/PE associate, consultant, product manager, or engineer who never lost touch with the customer

 - High Agency. You don't need someone to tell you what to do next.

 - Real fluency (or relentless hunger to develop it) in how investment firms operate - funds, GPs, LPs, capital calls, distributions, NAV

 - Comfort sitting across from a $5B fund CFO, being the smartest person in the room on AI workflows, and the most curious one on theirs

 - Bias to ship. You'd rather prototype something today than spec it for next quarter

 - Customer obsession that borders on irrational - you'll fly out for a 30-minute meeting if it earns trust


YOU MAY BE:

 - A former founder who scaled something 0→1 and wants to do it again

 - An ex investment banker or private equity associate who's tired of decks and wants to build

 - A product manager or engineer who loves customers more than tickets

 - A consultant (ex-McKinsey/Bain/BCG) ready to own outcomes instead of recommending them


THIS ISN'T THE RIGHT FIT IF

 - You need a customer s

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT HANOVER PARK

We're building the AI-native ERP that will define private markets infrastructure. This isn't an AI wrapper slapped onto legacy software - we built a proprietary general ledger accounting system from the ground up, designed specifically for complex investment firms. As the system of record, we've earned the trust to bundle fund administration with tools that were previously fragmented: investor portals, AI-powered portfolio management, KPI collection, and more.

With AI, context is king. And Hanover Park has all the context on our customers.

In under a year, we've scaled to over $15 billion in assets under administration, serving investment firms and LPs that include top-5 banks and major endowments. We're backed by Emergence Capital (first investor in Salesforce and Zoom), Susa Ventures (first investor in Robinhood and Flexport), and Lux Capital.

Stripe for payments. Ramp for expenses. Hanover Park for investments.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Hanover Park is an engineering-first company on a mission to build the best engineering team. Our investors keep asking: "How are you shipping this much product?" You would join a builder culture.

No bureaucracy. No hierarchy. We care about shipping delightful products to our customers. Nothing else.

You'll work directly alongside our CTO, making technical decisions on the infrastructure behind billions of dollars in private markets assets - with real ownership over core systems, not features on a roadmap managed by someone else.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

 - AI-powered automation that cuts manual work out of fund administration: the workflows private equity, venture capital, and asset managers have complained about for 20 years

 - Full-stack products owned end-to-end - frontend, backend, infra, AI, deployment

 - Hard technical problems at the intersection of AI, finance, and accounting

 - Architecture and technical decisions alongside the CTO

No QA team. No PM writing detailed specs. You ship it, you own it.




STACK

TypeScript/Next.js, Supabase/Postgres, AWS, Trigger.dev http://Trigger.dev for background jobs and AI inference.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Need:

 - Strong full-stack fundamentals across frontend, backend, and infra

 - Production experience building or integrating LLM/AI workflows

 - High ownership - you don't wait for a spec to start solving a problem

Nice to have:

 - Fintech or accounting software background

 - Data-intensive backend work (healthtech, insurance, finance, loans)

 - Seed-to-Series B startup experience

This isn't the right fit if:

 - You need a PM to define the problem before you can start

 - You're used to dedicated QA and design support and struggle without it

 - You need multiple rounds of review before anything ships




WHY JOIN NOW

Private markets are a $100 trillion asset class that's never had infrastructure built for it. We're building it. The learning curve is steep, the velocity expectations are high, and the work actually matters.

If that sounds like your kind of problem, let's talk.




EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYMENT

Hanover Park is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected under applicable federal, state, or local law.

We are committed to building a team that reflects a diversity of backgrounds, perspectives, and skills. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any protected characteristic.

If you require a reasonable accommodation during the application or interview process, please contact us at support@hanoverpark.com.



Hanover Park is headquartered in the Gramercy Area in New York City. This role is on-site 5 days per week.

source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT HANOVER PARK

We're building the AI-native ERP that will define private markets infrastructure. This isn't an AI wrapper slapped onto legacy software - we built a proprietary general ledger accounting system from the ground up, designed specifically for complex investment firms. As the system of record, we've earned the trust to bundle fund administration with tools that were previously fragmented: investor portals, AI-powered portfolio management, KPI collection, and more.

With AI, context is king. And Hanover Park has all the context on our customers.

In under a year, we've scaled to over $15 billion in assets under administration, serving investment firms and LPs that include top-5 banks and major endowments. We're backed by Emergence Capital (first investor in Salesforce and Zoom), Susa Ventures (first investor in Robinhood and Flexport), and Lux Capital.

Stripe for payments. Ramp for expenses. Hanover Park for investments.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Finance software is notoriously ugly. Dense tables, clunky workflows, zero delight. We think that's a design problem - and a real opportunity.

You'll join our product design team to shape how fund CFOs, operations leads, and LPs interact with their most critical workflows. That means owning craft end-to-end: the visual language, the system, the research, and the pixels. You'll work directly with founders and engineers - not through a PM layer.

The domain is complex. Waterfall models, fund accounting, LP reporting - none of it is simple to design for. But you'll have direct access to real users who will tell you exactly what's broken about every tool they've ever used. That's rare.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Major product surfaces - The surfaces that matter most; how LPs engage, how funds come to life, how data tells a story - and everything we build from here

 - Our design system - extending and hardening the patterns that scale across a complex platform

 - User research directly with fund CFOs, ops leads, and LPs - synthesizing what you learn into product decisions

 - Implementation-ready specs that engineers can build from without a back-and-forth

 - The long-term visual language of Hanover Park




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Need:

 - A portfolio that shows how you think through complex, data-heavy interfaces - not just how they look

 - Strong visual craft - you sweat the details, ship pixel-perfect work, and have a point of view on what good looks like

 - Experience building or evolving a design system in a fast-moving product

 - Comfort going deep with users - running research, not just reading someone else's findings

 - Figma fluency and a track record of handing off specs engineers love

Nice to have:

 - Fintech, accounting software, or enterprise SaaS background

 - Experience designing at a seed-to-Series B startup

This isn't the right fit if:

 - You need heavy PM direction before you can get started

 - You treat design as primarily a visual discipline - our product is functionally complex and design has to serve that

 - You haven't worked closely with engineers before




WHY JOIN NOW

Private markets are a $100 trillion asset class running on outdated software. We're fixing that. The work is hard, the velocity is high, and what you ship will be used by some of the most sophisticated investors in the world.

If that sounds like your kind of problem, let's talk.




EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYMENT

Hanover Park is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected under applicable federal, state, or local law.

We are committed to building a team that reflects a diversity of backgrounds, perspectives, and skills. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any protected characteristic.

If you require a reasonabl

source_jobs.title: Fund Accountant (Associate, Controller, Director)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT HANOVER PARK

We're building the AI-native ERP that will define private markets infrastructure. This isn't an AI wrapper slapped onto legacy software - we built a proprietary general ledger accounting system from the ground up, designed specifically for complex investment firms. As the system of record, we've earned the trust to bundle fund administration with tools that were previously fragmented: investor portals, AI-powered portfolio management, KPI collection, and more.

With AI, context is king. And Hanover Park has all the context on our customers.

In under a year, we've scaled to over $15 billion in assets under administration, serving investment firms and LPs that include top-5 banks and major endowments. We're backed by Emergence Capital (first investor in Salesforce and Zoom), Susa Ventures (first investor in Robinhood and Flexport), and Lux Capital.

Stripe for payments. Ramp for expenses. Hanover Park for investments.




ABOUT THE ROLE

You'll be a fund accountant who operates with the leverage of a team ten times your size - because AI is handling the grunt work. At Hanover Park, you won't spend your career buried in spreadsheets doing work that a model can do better and faster. You'll work on engagement teams, serve as the primary point of contact for sophisticated clients, and help shape how fund accounting gets done for the next decade.

Client capacity at Hanover Park is directly gated by this team. This hire matters.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Be a member of the engagement team for 5-10 clients and serve as the primary point of contact for those clients

 - Manage core fund accounting workflows: Maintaining books and records, capital calls and distributions, allocations and waterfalls, and capital account statements

 - Deliver financial reporting that meet client SLAs

 - Communicate directly with fund CFOs, COOs, and GPs — responsiveness and clarity are non-negotiable

 - Integrate into AI-assisted workflows and actively use automation to reduce time on manual tasks

 - Surface process improvement opportunities to the engineering team

 - Interface regularly with Chris and leadership, especially in early-stage client relationships




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Must-haves

 - Bachelor's degree in Accounting or a related field

 - Fund accounting experience in alternative investments

 - Fluency in core concepts: Fund structures, LPA terms, accounting and industry standards

 - Technical skills including, but not limited to AI/Claude

 - Strong client-facing communication skills - you're talking directly to CFOs and COOs

Nice-to-haves

 - Venture capital, private equity, private credit expertise

 - CPA or CPA candidate

 - Big 4 public accounting background

 - Genuine curiosity about AI and how it can transform fund administration workflows

This is not the role for you if:

 - You're purely execution-oriented with no interest in how AI can improve your work

 - You see services as lower-status than software — we disagree, and so should you

 - Responsiveness and clear communication aren't strengths




WHY JOIN NOW

You'll have direct exposure to some of the most innovative investment firms in the country, and you'll help define how modern fund accounting actually works. The AI infrastructure we've built means your capacity isn't limited by manual process — it's limited by judgment, and that's exactly where we want you spending your time.

We're building from scratch — there's no legacy playbook. The pace is fast and client expectations are high. If that's the environment where you do your best work, we should talk.




EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYMENT

Hanover Park is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected under applicable federal, state, or local law.

We are committed to building a te

source_jobs.title: Operations Associate (Deployment)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT HANOVER PARK

We're building the AI-native ERP that will define private markets infrastructure. This isn't an AI wrapper slapped onto legacy software - we built a proprietary general ledger accounting system from the ground up, designed specifically for complex investment firms. As the system of record, we've earned the trust to bundle fund administration with tools that were previously fragmented: investor portals, AI-powered portfolio management, KPI collection, and more.

With AI, context is king. And Hanover Park has all the context on our customers.

In under a year, we've scaled to over $15 billion in assets under administration, serving investment firms and LPs that include top-5 banks and major endowments. We're backed by Emergence Capital (first investor in Salesforce and Zoom), Susa Ventures (first investor in Robinhood and Flexport), and Lux Capital.

Stripe for payments. Ramp for expenses. Hanover Park for investments.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Our #1 objective is for Hanover Park customers to be raving fans. From the moment they sign a contract to the launch of their firm, we want that experience to be filled with magical moments.

You will own migration and implementation for the most complex investment firms in the world - moving them from legacy, disconnected systems onto Hanover Park's system of record. This isn't back-office work. You'll be on the front line of some of the most consequential client relationships in private markets, with real ownership from day one.

At Hanover Park, "services is sales." This role directly drives retention and expansion on our path to becoming a billion-dollar company.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Own client migration and implementation end-to-end — from signed contract through successful launch

 - Build and continuously improve core operational processes and playbooks

 - Collaborate with fund accountants and world-class engineers to ensure operational precision and exceptional service delivery

 - Execute detailed quality control and reconciliation across historical customer financial reporting periods

 - Troubleshoot issues, identify patterns, and provide data-driven recommendations for operational improvements

 - Surface process improvements proactively and work with engineering to build them into the product




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Must-haves

 - Exceptional organizational and project management skills - you can track many moving pieces simultaneously without dropping the ball

 - Strong written and verbal communication - you represent Hanover Park directly with institutional clients, fund CFOs, and COOs

 - Bias toward action - you don't wait for permission or a perfect playbook to solve a problem

 - 1–7 years in fund accounting, fund administration, or investment operations, ideally supporting private equity, venture capital, private credit, or other alternative investment vehicles

 - Deep understanding of fund structures, capital calls and distributions, NAV calculations, and financial reporting across periods

Nice-to-haves

 - Prior experience in PE/VC operations or fund administration

 - CPA preferred but not required - candidates with strong practical fund accounting experience are equally valued

 - Exposure to client onboarding or data migration workflows


WHY JOIN NOW

You won't be doing meaningless ops work. You'll be solving one of the biggest problems at Hanover Park - delivering a client experience so good it becomes our strongest growth lever. You'll work closely with leadership on client escalations and strategic decisions, with direct exposure to Chris (CEO) and the founding team. The role is broad by design. Some days are strategic; some days are firefighting. Client expectations are high and timelines are tight. If that energizes you, we should talk.

You'll personally onboard the funds that determine whether Hanover Park becomes the AI-native system of record for private markets. Our first 100 customers shape the next 1,000.




EQUAL OPPORTU

source_jobs.title: Treasury Operations Associate
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT HANOVER PARK

We're building the AI-native ERP that will define private markets infrastructure. This isn't an AI wrapper slapped onto legacy software — we built a proprietary general ledger accounting system from the ground up, designed specifically for complex investment firms. As the system of record, we've earned the trust to bundle fund administration with tools that were previously fragmented: investor portals, AI-powered portfolio management, KPI collection, and more.

With AI, context is king. And Hanover Park has all the context on our customers.

In under a year, we've scaled to over $15 billion in assets under administration, serving investment firms and LPs that include top-5 banks and major endowments. We're backed by Emergence Capital (first investor in Salesforce and Zoom), Susa Ventures (first investor in Robinhood and Flexport), and Lux Capital.

Stripe for payments. Ramp for expenses. Hanover Park for investments.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Capital calls, distributions, and wire transfers are zero-error-tolerance workflows. You'll be the financial control layer for some of the most sophisticated investment firms in the country — and you'll do it at a company that's actively automating the most painful parts of your job.This isn't a role where you inherit a legacy playbook and execute it indefinitely. You'll help document, streamline, and eventually automate the treasury processes that keep our clients' capital moving accurately and on time. You'll work closely with our fund accounting and engineering teams to make sure the operational infrastructure keeps pace with our growth.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Process and own the full range of money movement workflows — capital calls, distributions, investment wires, bill payments, intercompany payables/reimbursements, and management fee payments, ensuring that they are processed accurately and on time

 - Serve as the operational control layer, catching errors before they reach the client

 - Reconcile cash positions daily and maintain current, accurate records

 - Partner with engineering to document and systematize treasury processes

 - Identify and surface automation opportunities to reduce manual intervention

 - Support fund accounting leadership on client escalations when needed

 - Own treasury approval workflows — helping migrate approvals out of ad hoc channels and into structured, auditable processes within the platform




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Must-haves

 - Hands-on experience processing wire transfers and capital call/distribution workflows

 - Extreme attention to detail — you catch errors before anyone else does

 - Ability to work quickly under deadline pressure without sacrificing accuracy

Nice-to-haves

 - Experience at a fund administrator, PE/VC back office, or bank treasury team

 - Familiarity with fund accounting platforms

 - Understanding of management fee and waterfall structures

This is not the role for you if:

 - You're comfortable letting errors slide until someone else catches them

 - You've only worked in highly manual, paper-based processes and have no interest in automation

 - Attention to detail isn't something people would use to describe you




WHY JOIN NOW

You'll help design the future of fund treasury ops — not just execute a legacy workflow. At Hanover Park, the most tedious parts of your job are exactly what we're building AI to eliminate. That means your time goes toward higher-judgment work, and your impact compounds as the platform scales.

The stakes are real. Errors have consequences for clients and their LPs. If that kind of accountability motivates rather than intimidates you, we should talk.




EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYMENT

Hanover Park is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected under applicab

source_jobs.title: Founding Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT HANOVER PARK

Hanover Park http://hanoverpark.com builds AI systems that run the financial operations of the world’s most sophisticated investment firms. As an AI-enabled service https://www.emcap.com/thoughts/the-death-of-deloitte-ai-enabled-services-are-opening-a-whole-new-market and fund administrator https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fund_administration, we’ve become the way fund CFOs adopt AI. We've raised $31 million from Emergence https://www.emcap.com/ (investor in Salesforce/Zoom), Susa https://www.susaventures.com/ (investor in Robinhood/Flexport) and angel investor fund CFOs from Bain Capital https://www.baincapital.com/ ($180B), Clearlake https://clearlake.com/ ($100B), CD&R https://www.cdr.com/ ($80B), and Hellman & Friedman https://hf.com/ ($80B).

Our mission is to be the financial infrastructure to orchestrate how the world’s capital is operated, governed and allocated. Stripe for payments. Ramp for expenses. Hanover Park for investments. In the next decade, the AI systems we build will advise $100B investment firms on their largest acquisitions and eventually unlock alpha for investors.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're building the Navy SEALs of go-to-market: a small, elite team where every operator carries the full weight of the mission. No SDR layer to lean on. No marketing engine to blame. No sales ops layering process between you and the customer.

To date, our CEO Chris has scaled sales singlehandedly - from zero to $15B, with top-10 banks and major endowments on the platform. Our pipeline is the problem most companies dream about: inbound demand is overwhelming and we can't keep up. Real money is sitting on the table because we don't have enough trusted operators to run it down.

That's where you come in. As a Founding Account Executive, you'll work shoulder-to-shoulder with Chris - sitting in on his calls, taking over his accounts, and building the sales motion that the next ten AEs will run on. You're the second sales hire, with the same standard of ownership.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Closing inbound demand from VC, PE, and private credit GPs - funds ranging from emerging managers to multi-billion-dollar platforms

 - Building a repeatable outbound motion that includes developing relationships with the most important CFOs in VC, private equity and private credit

 - Running the full cycle: discovery, demo, technical deep-dives with CFOs, pricing, contract, signature, handoff to onboarding

 - Sitting next to Chris on his live deals, then taking them over - and building the playbook as you go so the next AE can ramp in weeks, not quarters

 - Owning a quota that matters. We're not assigning territories yet because we don't need to - the demand is there. Your number is real revenue against real pipeline

 - Partnering directly with product and engineering. When a prospect needs something to close, you figure out whether it ships this week or never

No SDRs. No deal desk. No solutions engineer. You demo it, you scope it, you close it, you own it.

What we're looking for:

 - Real fluency with how investment firms operate — funds, GPs, LPs, capital calls, distributions, NAV - you were likely an investment banker or private equity associate

 - 2+ years closing complex B2B deals, ideally to financial services buyers - fund admin, fintech, accounting software, ERP, or vertical SaaS into asset managers

 - A track record of being the top performer on a small team, not the median performer on a big one

 - High ownership. You don't wait for a deal review to figure out what to do next

 - Comfort selling alongside a founder and getting out-prepared by the customer's CFO if you're not sharp

Nice to have:

 - Sold into private markets (fund admins, portfolio monitoring, LP reporting, GP tech)

 - Founding or first-five GTM experience at a Seed-to-Series B startup

 - Existing relationships with VC, PE, or private credit GPs and their CFOs/COOs

This isn't the right fit if:

 - You need an SDR to book your meetings

source_jobs.title: Founding GTM Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
About Hanover Park

Hanover Park http://hanoverpark.com/ builds AI systems that run the financial operations of the world’s most sophisticated investment firms. As an AI-enabled service https://www.emcap.com/thoughts/the-death-of-deloitte-ai-enabled-services-are-opening-a-whole-new-market and fund administrator https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fund_administration, we’ve become the way fund CFOs adopt AI. We've raised $31 million from Emergence https://www.emcap.com/ (investor in Salesforce/Zoom), Susa https://www.susaventures.com/ (investor in Robinhood/Flexport) and angel investor fund CFOs from Bain Capital https://www.baincapital.com/ ($180B), Clearlake https://clearlake.com/ ($100B), CD&R https://www.cdr.com/ ($80B), and Hellman & Friedman https://hf.com/ ($80B).

Our mission is to be the financial infrastructure to orchestrate how the world’s capital is operated, governed and allocated. Stripe for payments. Ramp for expenses. Hanover Park for investments. In the next decade, the AI systems we build will advise $100B investment firms on their largest acquisitions and eventually unlock alpha for investors.

 
 

The role

Engineering is covered. Now the business side has to catch up.

Sales, operations and scaling fund accounting/fund services - every non-technical hire that makes Hanover Park run is sitting in this seat. Hiring has been distributed across the leadership team and it's worked, but it doesn't scale. Referrals are pouring in and real business talent is sitting on the table because no one owns the function end-to-end.

As our Founding Recruiter for Business & Operations, you're the first person at Hanover Park whose full-time job is to scale every non-technical team. You own every search outside of engineering, you build the playbook every future recruiter will run on, and you turn Hanover Park into a real recruiting machine.

 
 

What you'll work on

 - Full-cycle hiring across all non-technical functions including fund accounting/fund services which is 40% of our current team

 - Sourcing sharp ICs at the Associate-to-Senior level who've operated inside high-growth fintech, financial services, or B2B SaaS

 - The playbook — interview loops, scorecards, sourcing channels, outreach, offer benchmarks — built from scratch

 - Pipeline health across every open search: pass-through rates, time-to-offer, offer acceptance

 
 

What we're looking for

Need:

 - 5+ years recruiting business, operations, or go-to-market talent — ideally into fintech, financial services, or B2B SaaS

 - Genuine breadth — comfortable opening a finance search in the morning and a sales search in the afternoon

 - High ownership — if a candidate goes cold Friday, you're texting them Sunday

 - Instinct for quality — you know within one conversation whether someone has actually done the work or just sat near it

Nice to have:

 - Experience building recruiting infrastructure from scratch at a seed-to-Series B company

 - Familiarity with Ashby or a comparable ATS

 
 

This isn't the right fit if

 - You need a sourcing team to build your top of funnel

 - You want a 12-month ramp period

 - You hand candidates off to a coordinator after the first call

 
 

Why now

Private markets are a $100 trillion asset class running on outdated software. We're fixing that — and the team we hire in the next 12 months is the determining factor. The inbound pipeline is full, the brand wins candidates, and the next recruiter inherits the playbook you build.

If that sounds like your kind of problem, let's talk.

 
 

Equal opportunity

Hanover Park is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic.

We're committed to building a team with diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills. All qualified applicants will be considered regardless of protected status.

For accommodations dur

source_jobs.title: Chief of Staff
source_jobs.description:
About Hanover Park

Hanover Park http://hanoverpark.com builds AI systems that run the financial operations of the world’s most sophisticated investment firms. As an AI-enabled service https://www.emcap.com/thoughts/the-death-of-deloitte-ai-enabled-services-are-opening-a-whole-new-market and fund administrator https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fund_administration, we’ve become the way fund CFOs adopt AI. We've raised $31 million from Emergence https://www.emcap.com/ (investor in Salesforce/Zoom), Susa https://www.susaventures.com/ (investor in Robinhood/Flexport) and angel investor fund CFOs from Bain Capital https://www.baincapital.com/ ($180B), Clearlake https://clearlake.com/ ($100B), CD&R https://www.cdr.com/ ($80B), and Hellman & Friedman https://hf.com/ ($80B).

Our mission is to be the financial infrastructure to orchestrate how the world's capital is operated, governed and allocated. Stripe for payments. Ramp for expenses. Hanover Park for investments. In the next decade, the AI systems we build will advise $100B investment firms on their largest acquisitions and eventually unlock alpha for investors.



The role

We're ~40 people scaling to ~100 by year end. Chris (CEO) is running sales, product, and the company at the same time. That doesn't scale.

Right now, too many cross-functional priorities live in Chris's head. Initiatives get started and stall. Owners aren't always clear. Follow-through is inconsistent not because the team isn't good, but because no one's job is to make sure things land.

As Chief of Staff, you're the connective tissue between Chris's priorities and the org's execution. You run the operating system that keeps a fast-growing company from losing things in the gaps.

This is a high-trust, high-visibility role. You'll have more context on what matters and why than almost anyone at the company.



What you'll work on

 - Launching special projects end to end: scoping, staffing, tracking, and closing them out

 - Owning staffing and pod allocation across services teams

 - Building capacity, utilization, and performance visibility for fund accounting and services

 - Turning founder priorities into owners, deadlines, and execution plans

 - Running follow-through on cross-functional initiatives across product, recruiting, finance, and ops

 - Resolving operational loose ends before they escalate to Chris

 - Owning launch readiness and outstanding-item tracking across the business

 - Driving and owning the company financial model

What we're looking for

The ideal background is an ex-founder, a chief of staff at a hypergrowth company, or an ex-investment banker who wants to operate rather than advise.

Nice to have:
